No Preview Image When You Share to LINE or Discord? Force-Refresh Social OG Cache with ?v=1

This post starts with the classic problem of sharing your blog post to a social platform and getting a stale preview image, then walks through Open Graph tags, how platforms cache og:image / og:title / og:description, why Facebook / X / LINE / Discord still show old content after you update your OG tags, and how to force a refresh by appending a cache-busting query string like `?v=1` `?v=2`. Also covers how to use the Facebook Sharing Debugger, rough cache lifetimes by platform, what to do about Twitter / X's Card Validator being retired in 2022, and whether `?v=1` impacts SEO or canonical URLs.
Build Your First LINE Bot with Node.js — Using OpenAI GPT-3 and GPT-3.5 as Examples

OpenAI's ChatGPT has been blowing up recently, and OpenAI also exposes an API we can integrate with. This post focuses on GPT-3 but also covers how to switch to ChatGPT API. We'll use Node.js to build a LINE chatbot backed by OpenAI.
Clear LINE's Link Preview Cache With Page Poker

LINE is everywhere as a sharing channel, but once a link's been shared, LINE caches the preview aggressively — and there's no obvious way to bust the cache. Until you discover Page Poker. This post covers LINE's official Page Poker tool for clearing link preview cache, plus Facebook's Sharing Debugger as the equivalent for Facebook.
